raw_response: Disagree with the chosen answer; while some may argue for evaluating "same individuals" in terms of age categories (option C), which indirectly compares similar demographic groupings, true cross-sectional studies involve sampling different individuals who represent various age cohorts within the defined population segment being studied. This aligns more closely with Option D, ensuring broad representation and comparability across distinct age groups. Thus, my revised answer would be D, reflecting the core definition where cross-sectionals aim to gather information from differing individuals representing varying age ranges during a specified period. Answer Choice: D.
